title: Perspectives on Religious and Spiritual Coping among Rural Grandparent Caregivers
abstract: Poverty and poor health disproportionately affect older adults serving as primary caregivers to their grandchildren. In underserved, rural regions, where grandparent caregivers may be especially vulnerable, grandparent caregivers may find support in cultural traditions. As part of a mixed methods study investigating stress among grandparent caregivers, the role religion and spirituality play in coping was explored using a survey complemented by semi-structured interviews with 26 rural grandparent caregivers. Findings suggest religion and spirituality facilitate coping by (a) providing a sense of purpose and perspective; (b) fostering peace and perseverance; and (c) promoting stability and social cohesion. This study describes how cultural assets aid grandparent caregivers' coping and may inform supportive policies and practices for grandfamilies.
authors_text: Dunfee, Madeline Nicole; Brown, Robyn Lewis; Schoenberg, Nancy E.
